segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Producing enough energy to meet our needs has become a serious problem.
segments.zh: 生产足够的能源来满足我们的需求已经成为一个严重的问题。由于世界人口增长和工业扩张，需求正在迅速上升。燃烧化石燃料，如天然气、煤炭和石油，严重破坏环境，而且它们最终会耗尽。多年来，科学家们一直在研究如何从可再生能源（如太阳能和风能）中获取能源，同时避免污染。今天我将概述海洋可再生能源——也称为海洋能——它利用海洋的运动。海洋可再生能源可分为三大类：波浪能、潮汐能和海洋热能转换，我将分别简要介绍。首先是波浪能。人们发明了许多装置来收集波浪能，例如“波浪龙”、“企鹅”和“巨鲸”等，研究正在进行中，试图找到一种真正高效的方法。这种能源潜力巨大，因为来源是持续的，而且波浪不会停止。发电可以通过使用水库的陆上系统或海上系统进行。但海洋波浪的问题是它们不稳定，风使它们向各个方向移动。这增加了创造高效技术的难度：理想情况下，所有波浪都应沿着同一条直线平稳、有规律地移动。另一个缺点是，海底的沙子和其他沉积物可能会被阻止正常流动，从而导致环境问题。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Demand is rising rapidly, because of the world's increasing population and expanding industry.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Burning fossil fuels, like gas, coal and oil, seriously damages the environment and they'll eventually run out.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: For a number of years now, scientists have been working out how we can derive energy from renewable sources, such as the sun and wind, without causing pollution.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Today I'll outline marine renewable energy – also called ocean energy – which harnesses the movement of the oceans.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Marine renewable energy can be divided into three main categories: wave energy, tidal energy and ocean thermal energy conversion, and I'll say a few words about each one.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: First, wave energy.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Numerous devices have been invented to harvest wave energy, with names such as Wave Dragon, the Penguin and Mighty Whale, and research is going on to try and come up with a really efficient method.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This form of energy has plenty of potential, as the source is constant, and there's no danger of waves coming to a standstill.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Electricity can be generated using onshore systems, using a reservoir, or offshore systems.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But the problem with ocean waves is that they're erratic, with the wind making them travel in every direction.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This adds to the difficulty of creating efficient technology: ideally all the waves would travel smoothly and regularly along the same straight line.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Another drawback is that sand and other sediment on the ocean floor might be stopped from flowing normally, which can lead to environmental problems.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The second category of marine energy that I'll mention is tidal energy.
segments.zh: 我要提到的第二类海洋能源是潮汐能。利用潮汐而非波浪作为能源的一个主要优点是它是可预测的：我们知道未来几年涨潮和退潮的确切时间。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: One major advantage of using the tide, rather than waves, as a source of energy is that it's predictable: we know the exact times of high and low tides for years to come.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: For tidal energy to be effective, the difference between high and low tides needs to be at least five metres, and this occurs naturally in only about forty places on Earth.
segments.zh: 要使潮汐能有效，高潮和低潮之间的落差至少需要五米，而地球上只有大约四十个地方自然存在这种情况。但可以通过建造潮汐潟湖来创造合适的条件，潟湖是一个与海洋隔开的海水区域。目前的一个计划是在威尔士海岸建造一个潮汐潟湖。这将是在斯旺西湾内的一个水域，由从海岸延伸出来的U形防波堤或水坝遮蔽。防波堤将包含十六个水轮机，当潮水上涨时，水流冲过防波堤，激活水轮机，水轮机带动发电机发电。然后，在退潮的三个小时里，水被挡在防波堤内，增加了水位差，直到潟湖内的水位比公海高出几米。然后，为了释放储存的水，防波堤中的闸门被打开。水从潟湖中强力涌出，驱动防波堤中的水轮机反向旋转，再次产生数千兆瓦的电力。由于每天有两次涨潮，这个潟湖方案每天将发电四次，每24小时总共约14小时，足以供超过15万户家庭用电。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But the right conditions can be created by constructing a tidal lagoon, an area of sea water separated from the sea.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: One current plan is to create a tidal lagoon on the coast of Wales.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This will be an area of water within a bay at Swansea, sheltered by a U-shaped breakwater, or dam, built out from the coast.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The breakwater will contain sixteen hydro turbines, and as the tide rises, water rushes through the breakwater, activating the turbines, which turn a generator to produce electricity.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Then, for three hours as the tide goes out, the water is held back within the breakwater, increasing the difference in water level, until it's several metres higher within the lagoon than in the open sea.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Then, in order to release the stored water, gates in the breakwater are opened.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It pours powerfully out of the lagoon, driving the turbines in the breakwater in the opposite direction and again generating thousands of megawatts of electricity.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: As there are two high tides a day, this lagoon scheme would generate electricity four times a day, every day, for a total of around 14 hours in every 24 – and enough electricity for over 150,000 homes.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This system has quite a lot in its favour: unlike solar and wind energy it doesn't depend on the weather; the turbines are operated without the need for fuel, so it doesn't create any greenhouse gas emissions; and very little maintenance is needed.
segments.zh: 这个系统有很多优点：与太阳能和风能不同，它不依赖天气；涡轮机运行不需要燃料，因此不产生温室气体排放；而且几乎不需要维护。据估计，以这种方式发电将相对便宜，制造部件将创造超过2000个就业机会，对当地经济是一个巨大的推动。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It's estimated that electricity generated in this way will be relatively cheap, and that manufacturing the components would create more than 2,000 jobs, a big boost to the local economy.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: On the other hand, there are fears that lagoons might harm both fish and birds, for example by disturbing migration patterns, and causing a build-up of silt, affecting local ecosystems.
segments.zh: 另一方面，有人担心潟湖可能会伤害鱼类和鸟类，例如通过干扰迁徙模式，以及导致淤泥堆积，影响当地生态系统。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: There are other forms of tidal energy, but I'll go on to the third category of marine energy: ocean thermal energy conversion.
segments.zh: 还有其他形式的潮汐能，但我将继续介绍第三类海洋能源：海洋热能转换。这依赖于表层水和海面下几公里处的水之间存在很大的温差，这种情况发生在热带沿海地区。其想法是利用一根水下管道将冷水带到表面。这个概念可以追溯到1881年，当时……

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This depends on there being a big difference in temperature between surface water and the water a couple of kilometres below the surface, and this occurs in tropical coastal areas.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The idea is to bring cold water up to the surface using a submerged pipe.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The concept dates back to 1881, when ...

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: There are other forms of tidal energy, but I'll go on to the third category of marine energy: ocean thermal energy conversion.
segments.zh: 还有其他形式的潮汐能，但我将继续介绍第三类海洋能源：海洋热能转换。这依赖于表层水和海面下几公里处的水之间存在很大的温差，这种情况发生在热带沿海地区。其想法是利用一根水下管道将冷水带到表面。这个概念可以追溯到1881年，当时……

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This depends on there being a big difference in temperature between surface water and the water a couple of kilometres below the surface, and this occurs in tropical coastal areas.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The idea is to bring cold water up to the surface using a submerged pipe.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The concept dates back to 1881, when ...

items.prompt: More energy required because of growth in population and 31

pairs.note: growth 替换为 increasing，意思相同。
More energy required 对应 Demand is rising rapidly，表达需求增长。
原文中 industry 前有 expanding，但核心词 industry 直接对应。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问的是人口增长和什么导致需要更多能源，定位句提到 Demand is rising rapidly, because of the world's increasing population and expanding industry，所以答案是 industry。

items.prompt: waves provide a 32 source of renewable energy

pairs.note: 题干说'可再生能源的来源'，原文说'来源是恒定的'，constant替换了32题要填的词。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'this form of energy has plenty of potential, as the source is constant'，其中'source is constant'对应题干'waves provide a constant source of renewable energy'，所以答案填constant。

pairs.note: any 和 every 都表示‘每一个’，是同义替换。
direction 在原文中直接出现，是原词复现。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问‘waves can move in any 33’，定位句说‘the wind making them travel in every direction’，其中‘every direction’对应‘any direction’，因此答案填direction。

items.prompt: movement of sand, etc. on the 34 of the ocean may be affected

pairs.note: sand 对应 sand, etc. 对应 other sediment
题干中 34 是题号，实际填 floor，原文直接出现 ocean floor

pairs.p: might be stopped from flowing normally
pairs.note: may be affected 被具体化为 might be stopped from flowing normally

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 sand and other sediment on the ocean floor might be stopped from flowing normally，其中 ocean floor 对应题干中的 on the 34 of the ocean，因此答案填 floor。

pairs.note: 题干中的'more...than'比较结构，原文用'rather than'表达对比，强调潮汐比波浪更可预测。
题干关键词'predictable'在原文中直接出现，是定位答案的核心词。

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干中的比较结构'more...than'和关键词'predictable'，在原文中找到对应的'rather than'和'predictable'，从而定位答案。

items.prompt: will be created in a 36 at Swansea

pairs.note: created in 被替换为 within，意思相同。
题干中的 36 对应原文中的 bay，是答案所在。
地点信息完全一致，帮助定位。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 'within a bay at Swansea'，其中 'bay' 是题干中 36 的答案，且 'at Swansea' 与题干一致，直接定位。

items.prompt: stored water is released through 37 , driving the turbines in the reverse direction

pairs.p: gates in the breakwater are opened
pairs.note: 原文中'gates in the breakwater are opened'对应题干'stored water is released through'，'gates'就是答案。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'Then, in order to release the stored water, gates in the breakwater are opened.'，其中'gates'直接对应题干中的'through'后的空格。

items.prompt: no 38 is required to make it work

pairs.q: no ... is required to make it work
pairs.note: 题干说‘不需要某物来工作’，原文说‘不需要燃料’，意思相同。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问‘不需要什么来使其工作’，原文定位句提到‘turbines are operated without the need for fuel’，所以答案就是fuel。

items.prompt: likely to create a number of 39

pairs.note: 原文直接使用'create'，与题干'create'完全一致。
题干中的'39'是题号，答案对应原文的'jobs'，但题干'create a number of'与原文'create...jobs'匹配。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中'create a number of'与原文'create...jobs'对应，其中'jobs'是答案。

items.prompt: may harm fish and birds, e.g. by affecting 40 and building up silt

pairs.note: affecting 和 disturbing 都表示'影响'，但 disturbing 更强调干扰。
原文是 causing a build-up of silt，题干是 building up silt，意思相同。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的 affecting 对应原文的 disturbing，building up silt 对应原文的 causing a build-up of silt，答案 migration 在原文中出现在 disturbing migration patterns 中。
